Exploring Our World: Geography & Maps
LanternELLCover📄 Printable Pack
EN-ES Printable Resource

Exploring Our World: Geography & Maps

This vocabulary pack introduces 3rd-5th grade ELL newcomers to essential geography and map terms in English and Spanish. It includes a mini-book, interactive worksheets, and teacher notes to support learning.

TopicGeography & Maps
Age Band3-5
Pack Typevocabulary pack
LanguageEN-ES

✨ What's Inside

  • 15 vocabulary items
  • 3 worksheets
  • Mini-book (8 pages)
  • Teacher notes & answer key

📖 How to Use

Introduce vocabulary using flashcards or images. Read the mini-book aloud, pausing to discuss concepts and check for understanding. Complete the matching worksheet as a class or in small groups. Use the tracing worksheet for handwriting practice and vocabulary reinforcement. The coloring worksheet provides a hands-on activity to apply learned terms. Encourage students to point out map features on a classroom map or globe.

LanternELLMini-Book 1/2📄 Printable Pack

Mini-Book: My Geography Book / Mi Libro de Geografía

Print double-sided, fold in half, and staple to create a mini-book.

1
My Geography Book
Mi Libro de Geografía
2
What is a Map? A map is a drawing of a place. It shows us where things are.
¿Qué es un Mapa? Un mapa es un dibujo de un lugar. Nos muestra dónde están las cosas.
3
What is a Globe? A globe is a model of Earth. It is shaped like a ball.
¿Qué es un Globo Terráqueo? Un globo terráqueo es un modelo de la Tierra. Tiene forma de pelota.
4
Continents and Oceans. Earth has 7 continents (big pieces of land) and 5 oceans (big bodies of water).
Continentes y Océanos. La Tierra tiene 7 continentes (grandes extensiones de tierra) y 5 océanos (grandes cuerpos de agua).
Mar 18, 2026lanternell.comPage 6 / 13
LanternELLMini-Book 2/2📄 Printable Pack
5
Compass Rose. A compass rose shows directions: North, South, East, West. This helps us know where we are going.
Rosa de los Vientos. Una rosa de los vientos muestra las direcciones: Norte, Sur, Este, Oeste. Esto nos ayuda a saber adónde vamos.
6
Map Legend. A map legend, or key, tells us what the symbols on a map mean. For example, a tree might mean a forest.
Leyenda del Mapa. Una leyenda del mapa, o clave, nos dice qué significan los símbolos en un mapa. Por ejemplo, un árbol podría significar un bosque.
7
Landforms. Maps show different landforms like tall mountains, long rivers, and big lakes. They also show deserts and forests.
Formas Terrestres. Los mapas muestran diferentes formas terrestres como montañas altas, ríos largos y lagos grandes. También muestran desiertos y bosques.
8
Why use Maps? Maps help us find places, plan trips, and understand our world!
¿Por qué usar Mapas? ¡Los mapas nos ayudan a encontrar lugares, planificar viajes y entender nuestro mundo!

LanternELLWorksheet 3📄 Printable Pack

Worksheet 3

coloring
Name: ____________________

Color the map! Use blue for the lake and river, green for the forest, brown for the mountain, and yellow for the desert. Then, label each feature using the words provided.

¡Colorea el mapa! Usa azul para el lago y el río, verde para el bosque, marrón para la montaña y amarillo para el desierto. Luego, etiqueta cada característica usando las palabras dadas.

🖍️ Color this scene

LanternELLTeacher Notes📄 Printable Pack

Teacher Notes

🎯 Objective

Students will be able to identify and name common geography terms and map features (e.g., map, globe, continent, ocean, river, mountain, compass rose, legend) in English and Spanish. Students will understand the basic purpose of a map.

💡 Suggested Use

Introduce vocabulary using flashcards or images. Read the mini-book aloud, pausing to discuss concepts and check for understanding. Complete the matching worksheet as a class or in small groups. Use the tracing worksheet for handwriting practice and vocabulary reinforcement. The coloring worksheet provides a hands-on activity to apply learned terms. Encourage students to point out map features on a classroom map or globe.

📦 Materials Needed

  • Printed vocabulary cards (optional)
  • Printed mini-books (one per student)
  • Printed worksheets (one per student)
  • Crayons, colored pencils, or markers
  • Pencils
  • Scissors (for mini-book assembly)
  • Glue or stapler (for mini-book assembly)
  • Large world map or globe for classroom reference

🔀 Differentiation Tips

  • **For struggling learners:** Pre-teach key vocabulary, pair with a stronger student for activities, provide sentence starters for discussions, use realia (e.g., a small globe) for hands-on exploration.
  • **For advanced learners:** Encourage them to find specific countries/states on a map, research a specific landform, or draw their own simple map of a familiar place (e.g., their classroom, school).
  • **Visual Support:** Emphasize the image prompts and provide clear visual aids for all vocabulary words.
  • **L1 Support:** Allow students to use their L1 for initial understanding and discussion before transitioning to English.

LanternELLAnswer Key 1📄 Printable Pack

Answer Key

ws-1

1.Mapa
2.Globo terráqueo
3.Continente
4.Océano
5.Río
6.Montaña
7.Ciudad

ws-2

Tracing is subjective, but students should accurately trace the words 'MAP', 'GLOBE', 'RIVER', 'MOUNTAIN', 'OCEAN' and draw a relevant simple picture for each.

Mar 18, 2026lanternell.comPage 12 / 13
LanternELLAnswer Key 2📄 Printable Pack

Answer Key

ws-3

Students should color the map features correctly (blue for lake/river, green for forest, brown for mountain, yellow for desert) and accurately label the corresponding features with the provided words.
